subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – Say the Right Thing
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: A girl named Anya was very shy. Whenever she met new people, she stayed quiet because she was afraid of saying the wrong thing. Her mother told her that kind words are like seeds that grow into friendships.
questions: Who is the main character?
How is Anya described?
Why did she stay quiet when meeting new people?
What did her mother compare kind words to?
What do these 'seeds' grow into?

passages.paragraph: One day at school, Anya saw her friend Leo looking sad. His drawing had accidentally been torn. Anya wanted to help, so she walked over to him and thought carefully about what to say.
questions: Where was Anya?
Who was looking sad?
What happened to Leo's drawing?
What did Anya want to do?
How did she think before speaking?

passages.paragraph: Anya remembered that saying the right thing means being honest and gentle. She told Leo, 'I am so sorry about your drawing. It was very beautiful.' Leo looked up and wiped his tears.
questions: What two things does 'saying the right thing' mean?
What did Anya tell Leo about his drawing?
How did she describe the drawing?
What did Leo do after Anya spoke?
Was Anya's comment mean or kind?

passages.paragraph: Later, in the playground, a boy named Tom fell down while running. Some children started to laugh. Anya knew that laughing was not the right thing to do, so she stepped forward to help him up.
questions: Where did the second event happen?
What happened to Tom?
What did some children do when Tom fell?
Did Anya think laughing was right?
What did she do for Tom?

passages.paragraph: Anya said to Tom, 'Are you okay? That was a brave run!' Tom stopped feeling embarrassed and thanked her. Anya realized that the right words can make a person feel much better and stronger.
questions: What did Anya ask Tom?
How did she describe his run?
How did Tom feel after Anya spoke?
What did Tom say to Anya?
What can the right words do for a person?

passages.paragraph: At lunch, Anya’s teacher, Ms. Green, told the class that words have power. She explained that we should always think before we speak. 'Once a word leaves your mouth, you cannot take it back,' she said.
questions: What was the teacher's name?
When did the teacher talk to the class?
What do words have, according to Ms. Green?
What should we do before we speak?
Can you take a word back once it is spoken?

passages.paragraph: Anya practiced saying 'please' and 'thank you' every day. She found that when she used polite words, people were happy to help her. Being polite is an important part of saying the right thing.
questions: Which two polite phrases did Anya practice?
How often did she practice them?
How did people feel when she was polite?
Is being polite important?
What did Anya find out about people's reactions?

passages.paragraph: One evening, Anya’s brother broke her favorite toy. She felt very angry. She wanted to yell, but she took a deep breath instead. She knew that angry words would only cause more trouble.
questions: Who broke Anya's toy?
How did Anya feel?
What did she want to do at first?
What did she do instead of yelling?
What do angry words cause?

passages.paragraph: Anya said, 'I am upset that my toy is broken, but I know it was an accident.' Her brother said he was sorry and offered to fix it. By saying the right thing, Anya turned a fight into a solution.
questions: How did Anya describe her feeling about the toy?
Did she think her brother broke it on purpose?
What did her brother say?
What did the brother offer to do?
What did Anya turn the fight into?

passages.paragraph: Anya was no longer the shy girl who stayed quiet. She learned that speaking up with kindness is a great gift. Saying the right thing helps everyone live together in peace and happiness.
questions: Was Anya still shy at the end?
What kind of gift is speaking up with kindness?
What does saying the right thing help people do?
What two feelings result from saying the right thing?
What did Anya learn in the end?
